summary: Severe preeclampsia is defined by either a 24-hour urinary protein excretion of at least 300 mg or a spot protein-to-creatinine ratio of ≥0.3. This criterion aligns with standard obstetric guidelines and serves as the definitive benchmark for diagnosing severe disease based on proteinuria levels.
